activities_projects.title: Ecosystem Dependency Model
steps: Choose a forest ecosystem
Identify producers, consumers, and decomposers
Create a diagram showing energy flow and ecological relationships
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and completeness of ecological model (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Biodiversity Data Interpretation
steps: Research biodiversity statistics of India
Create a table showing species diversity across regions
Analyze reasons for high biodiversity in certain regions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop analytical skills using biodiversity data

activities_projects.title: Wildlife Conservation Policy Review
steps: Research the Wildlife Protection Act of 1972
List key provisions of the act
Evaluate its impact on wildlife conservation in India
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand environmental policies and their impact

activities_projects.title: Case Study on Project Tiger
activities_projects.objective: Study a major wildlife conservation program
steps: Research the history of Project Tiger
Explain the reasons behind declining tiger population
Evaluate the effectiveness of the project
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop case study analysis skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical evaluation of conservation efforts (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Forest Management Strategy Comparison
steps: Compare Reserved, Protected, and Unclassed forests
Analyze advantages and limitations of each category
Suggest improvements for forest management
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students critically evaluate forest management strategies

activities_projects.title: Community-Based Conservation Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the role of communities in conservation
steps: Research the Chipko Movement
Explain its social and environmental impact
Compare it with another community conservation effort
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the power of grassroots movements

activities_projects.title: Sacred Groves Research Project
steps: Research sacred groves in India
Explain their ecological importance
Discuss how cultural traditions help protect biodiversity
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze cultural contributions to conservation

activities_projects.title: Environmental Problem Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Investigate a real environmental issue affecting wildlife
steps: Identify a wildlife conservation issue in India
Analyze causes and consequences
Propose realistic solutions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop problem-solving and research skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of investigation and proposed solutions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Joint Forest Management Evaluation
steps: Research the concept of Joint Forest Management
Analyze benefits for local communities and forests
Suggest improvements for better participation

activities_projects.title: Sustainable Development Proposal
steps: Choose a forest region in India
Identify threats to biodiversity in that region
Propose a sustainable development plan balancing conservation and human needs
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students apply environmental concepts to real-world planning
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and practicality of proposal (10 marks)
